comparison_note: All comparisons use completed days: yesterday (2026-07-22) is the run_date. Today is excluded as it is an incomplete day. Cell C Recharges compares yesterday against same-day-last-month and same-day-two-months-ago, plus MTD-vs-prior-MTD (same day-of-month cutoff, so periods are equal length).

flags: 0 recharges recorded for 2026-07-22 — total outage safety net triggered. Root cause: VW_CELLC_RECHARGES has NOT advanced — MAX(TRANSACTION_DATE) is still 2026-07-17, i.e. 5 days behind the 2026-07-22 run_date. This is the SAME stall first flagged on the 2026-07-19 run (when the lag was only 2 days) — the pipeline has made no further progress in 3 days. This is no longer a transient blip and needs urgent data-team escalation.
2026-07-22 recharge count (0) is >20% below same-day-last-month (2026-06-22: 16,267) and same-day-two-months-ago (2026-05-22: 19,356) — consistent with the same data lag.
2026-07-22 revenue (R0) is >20% below same-day-last-month (R258,660) and same-day-two-months-ago (R300,905) — consistent with the same data lag.

MTD vs prior-month MTD count: down 18.0% (314,765 vs 383,753) — exceeds 10% threshold. Affected by the same load lag: the underlying data only extends to 2026-07-17, so the current MTD figure understates the true month-to-date count by roughly 5 days' worth of transactions.
MTD vs prior-month MTD value: down 17.0% (R4,786,874 vs R5,766,435) — exceeds 10% threshold, same load-lag caveat as above.
Same-day last-month vs two-months-ago count: down 16.0% (16,267 vs 19,356) — exceeds 5% threshold.
Same-day last-month vs two-months-ago value: down 14.0% (R258,660 vs R300,905) — exceeds 5% threshold.

REVENUE_WHATSAPP_PURCHASES_VALUE/QUANTITY excluded from every sum above — MAX() ~1.7e19, orders of magnitude beyond every other revenue column, with an unresolved 'Unknown Policy!' on both columns per DESCRIBE TABLE. Needs data-team/Snowflake-admin follow-up before those two streams can be trusted or included.
Yesterday vs same day last month qty: down 77.0% (4,872 vs 21,174) — exceeds 20% threshold.
Yesterday vs same day last month value: down 81.1% (R50,204 vs R265,934) — exceeds 20% threshold.
Yesterday vs same day two months ago qty: down 83.1% (4,872 vs 28,806) — exceeds 20% threshold.
Yesterday vs same day two months ago value: down 85.2% (R50,204 vs R338,616) — exceeds 20% threshold.
Same-day last-month vs two-months-ago qty: down 26.5% (21,174 vs 28,806) — exceeds 5% threshold.
Same-day last-month vs two-months-ago value: down 21.5% (R265,934 vs R338,616) — exceeds 5% threshold.
MTD vs prior-month MTD qty: down 12.1% (480,971 vs 546,990) — exceeds 10% threshold.

Tenant breakdown (yesterday vs same day last month, value): uConnect: R258,435 → R43,917 (-83.0%); ME&YOU: R7,499 → R6,287 (-16.2%). Drop is concentrated in uConnect but ME&YOU is down too this time (unlike 2026-07-21's report, where ME&YOU had grown). MAX(TRANSACTION_DATE) confirms 2026-07-22 is not lagging (data is present for that date), and the low row-count/revenue pattern is now sustained across at least 3 consecutive days — row counts of 5,532 (07-20), 4,533 (07-21), and 4,086 (07-22), all far below 2026-06-22's 18,800 — so this reads as a real, sustained multi-day revenue decline rather than a load-lag artifact, and it is worsening day over day. Needs urgent business-side investigation.

Day 2-4 registration-activity cohort: down 48.2% vs trailing-7-day average (1,665 vs avg 3,213) — below the 60% drastic-drop threshold so not RED, but still well below normal and a partial continuation of the decline flagged on 2026-07-21 (which was down 63.2%). Underlying registration volumes for the 07-15 to 07-22 window show a clear rise-then-fall pattern (peaking 07-19 at 8,440 new subs in that cohort window, down to 3,604 by 07-22) — worth continued monitoring even though today's threshold isn't breached.

INFORMATIONAL (not a coded rule in this check): total SIMs in view dropped from 453,396 (2026-07-21 run) to 428,723 (this run) — a same-day drop of 24,673 (-5.4%). This isn't covered by this check's per-metric drastic-drop rules (cohort/registration-based, not total-count based) but is a materially large, sudden change. See the DIM Subscriber Alignment and Terminations checks below — it coincides with a large batch of terminations newly dated in May 2026 that appears to have just been loaded into the warehouse. Recommend confirming with the data team that this was an intentional backfill.
Spread of 1,025 across subscriber tables — VW_ACTIVE (428,723) vs MERGE (427,701) / DIM (427,698) — exceeds the 15-record threshold.

MAJOR: all three subscriber tables dropped in lockstep since the 2026-07-21 run — VW_ACTIVE_SUBSCRIPTIONS 453,396→428,723 (-24,673, -5.4%); DIM_SUBSCRIBERS 453,990→427,698 (-26,292, -5.8%); UCONNECT_MAY_MERGE (uConnect-only) 453,992→427,701 (-26,291, -5.8%). This lines up closely with the Terminations check below: the full-calendar-month May 2026 termination total (uConnect) jumped from 26,879 (as previously observed) to 44,676 in this run — a gain of 17,797 records now carrying a May TERMINATION_DATE, which reduces each of these 'still active' counts. Reads as a one-time backdated termination batch newly loaded/processed into the warehouse between the two runs, not an active data-integrity break — but needs data-team confirmation given the scale.
Duplicate ACCOUNT_NUMBER/ICCID check: 3 duplicated account numbers (6 rows) and 6 duplicated ICCIDs (38 rows) among active uConnect records — both well under the 100 threshold, no flag.
19,710 terminations MTD is below the (avg of last 2 months + 500) threshold of 20,661 — avg of 18,255 and 22,067 = 20,161.

MAJOR data-shape event, read the RED flag above with this caveat: the 2026-05 (two-months-ago) MTD figure used in this threshold's baseline jumped from 1,088 (as previously observed, through 05-21) to 22,067 (through 05-22, this run) — a 20,979 increase from a single additional day's cutoff. The FULL calendar month of May 2026 also grew, from 26,879 (previously observed) to 44,676 (this run, +17,797) — confirming a large batch of terminations dated in May was newly loaded/processed into UCONNECT_MAY_MERGE between the 2026-07-21 and 2026-07-22 runs, rather than terminations flowing in evenly. This means: (a) the baseline used above is artificially inflated by that backfill, so the RED flag may partly reflect a distorted two-months-ago comparison rather than a real slowdown in July termination processing, and (b) this same event is very likely the direct cause of the ~25-26k active-subscriber-count drop flagged in DIM Subscriber Alignment. Recommend confirming with the data team whether this was an intentional backfill before acting on either flag.

Known data-shape issue, still unresolved: termination dates continue to look batch-applied within a month rather than flowing evenly day-by-day, as evidenced again by this run's May jump — a day-of-month-cutoff MTD comparison, which works fine for recharges/sales, may not be a reliable read on this metric. Worth confirming with the data team whether termination processing runs in batches before trusting month-to-date trends here either way.
